title: How a flaky test exposed a Redis client use-after-free
excerpt: A handful of flaky tests turned out to be a use-after-free in the Redis client's C extension. Here's how we tracked it down.
content_html:
It can be hard to fix a bug even when it happens deterministically; replicating the circumstances that trigger it is often the hardest step. When a bug is non-deterministic this becomes harder: we need to execute the code that reproduces it many times in order to observe the behaviour, which adds significant time to our feedback loop. Harder still is when the cause of the bug happens prior to its manifestation —when data is corrupted but we don’t immediately notice it. Our usual tools focus on capturing the state of the system at the time a crash happens, which doesn’t tell us why the corruption happened in the first place, just that it happened.

This is the tale of a memory corruption bug we found in the redis-client library and what helped us get there.

Chapter 1: Bounce

Jump! Bounce! Down! Up!

Our story starts with David, an engineering manager on our Scalability team. One idle Tuesday, David saw a build of his had failed due to what appeared to be a number of flaky tests. He started a thread in Slack to alert others and included a screenshot from the Test Engine dashboard.

As a continuous integration company, we are intimately familiar with flaky tests —they are an inevitable part of any large codebase. Within 15 minutes, the team pulled the Andon Cord; these tests were so flaky they were preventing us deploying code to production. As David summarised it, “[the] priority is getting main unblocked”, so we worked to exclude the tests from our suite temporarily.

With the urgency reduced, David and the many other developers blocked by this hiccup were free to go back to their original tasks. David, though, decided to take a slightly deeper look by inspecting the reliability of these tests using Buildkite’s Test Engine reliability score, to see if he could correlate when they suddenly became unstable.

It seems the three worst flakeys went from ~100% reliable to… not around [2-4 days prior]. I’ve had a look through merged PRs to see if I can spot what might have contributed to that change, but can’t see anything obvious… Only thing that jumps out is the redis upgrade?

That Redis upgrade was done on the prior Friday afternoon by a staff engineer, and part time blog post narrator, Patrick Robinson. Patrick loves few things more than talking about himself in the third person; one of those things is getting deep into gnarly bugs.

Here’s our first clue: the Redis gem upgrade had gone smoothly with no hint of any production issues. At this point, though, a second hypothesis appeared: the tests were flaky because they depended on a certain sequence of events. See, the flaky tests were part of our feature test suite; they utilised not just RSpec but also a Selenium headless browser and a test environment comprising of a web/API server, database and Redis server. In these situations, race conditions between the different components are a common cause of flaky tests. Unfortunately, this would lead our investigators off track.

So the suggestion was to slap a sleep in between the test setup and assertions to see if it made the problem go away. This wasn’t a solution, but a way of establishing if the flakiness was the result of a WebSocket message taking slightly too long to arrive.

Chapter 2: Fade to Black

Life, it seems, will fade away
Drifting further every day
Getting lost within myself
Nothing matters, no one else

The following Monday, more tests were failing sporadically in the same file as those that had been skipped. Patrick worked with another of our staff engineers who could, unlike Patrick, write actual frontend code. Together they managed to build a reliable reproduction of the bug, although it took up to 30 minutes to produce a failure. After eliminating the possibility of a race condition where messages were delivered after the test suite had run, the team narrowed in on the interactions between ActionCable and Redis.

By adding additional logging, they found the subscribe command was being delivered to ActionCable but wasn’t being received by Redis. Other developers also reported WebSockets often failing in development, indicating it was not only the test environment that was impacted.

At the end of the third day of debugging, seemingly getting nowhere, Patrick got a very unusual error message, which he posted to Slack with the departing words “I think it’s time to give up for the night…”:

text

ruby(29632,0x2a9f1f000) malloc: Double free of object 0x2a9bb6740
ruby(29632,0x2a9f1f000) malloc: *** set a breakpoint in malloc_error_break to debug

This kind of exception is quite unusual and hints at how deep this bug goes. But the team hadn’t quite gathered enough information to recognise how or even if it related to the failure. Like an installment of the blockbuster movie series Knives Out, even with all the information, it still didn’t make sense.

And yet, with all the pieces on the table, this crime still truly appears impossible. ~ Benoit Blanc

Chapter 3: Everlong

Hello
I’ve waited here for you
Everlong

Successful debugging requires a good dose of skill and a pinch of luck. Late on the following Thursday afternoon, a message was posted in our engineering Slack channel that a build had triggered a segmentation fault. Attached to the build was a core dump.

The core dump was attached because one of our developers was previously trying to debug a crash in the test suite, and had written a plugin to look for the presence of a core dump and, if one was found, upload it to the relevant build as an artifact.

That means it’s time to introduce our third character, Rian, who during his tenure at Buildkite developed a reputation for finding and fixing incredibly obscure bugs (along with the infamous Doom pipeline). The stories are so great that despite Rian’s departure we tell them to new hires, who retell them to others and so on and so forth.

The core dump enabled Rian to inspect the state of the process at the time it crashed, akin to a black box in an aircraft crash investigation. The crash message itself indicated it happened inside the hiredis code, which is a C extension bundled into the redis-client gem. From this, he ascertained that the exception was triggered by a call to the C function memmove. While the source and destination addresses for this call looked sane, the size field was set to 0x00a0ffffffffffb6 , which is about 45 petabytes.

Based on this and the fact we’d been seeing other unexpected behaviour since upgrading the Redis gem, it was logical to conclude a memory corruption bug was present in the newest version.

By another stroke of luck, Rian had attended a conference talk earlier in the year called Finding and fixing memory safety bugs in C with ASAN.

With this information, Rian set to work getting the reproducible code running in a version of Ruby compiled with ASAN. It only took a couple of hours to get this running, and within 30 minutes ASAN reported a heap use-after-free. In order to provide concurrent execution, hiredis has two threads for each connection, one for reading and one for writing. The use-after-free was happening because in some scenarios the reader thread would free the writer buffer.

Epilogue: In the End

I’m surprised it got so far
Things aren’t the way they were before
You wouldn’t even recognize me anymore
Not that you knew me back then, but it all comes back to me in the end

Once we knew that there was memory corruption and, more importantly, where it was coming from, fixing it was relatively straightforward. Rian disabled using the C extension for Redis connections in test, development and production, and developed a reproducible test case that was reported upstream.

What enabled us to find it in the first place is the interesting part. A combination of deliberate application of our skills in building the necessary tooling, teamwork, and just the right amount of luck. Our tools, such as Test Engine and our coredump-artifact plugin, enabled us to identify the nature of the bug and, combined with using ASAN, where it manifested. Memory corruption bugs such as these can often take months to fix, because having a core dump generated by a segfault only tells you that memory was corrupted at some point in the past, but gives you few clues as to how it became corrupt.

No production data was harmed in the making of this story.

sentences.zh: 即使 bug 确实发生，也很难修复它；复制触发它的环境通常是最困难的一步。

sentences.en: When a bug is non-deterministic this becomes harder: we need to execute the code that reproduces it many times in order to observe the behaviour, which adds significant time to our feedback loop.
sentences.zh: 当错误是不确定的时，这会变得更加困难：我们需要多次执行重现该错误的代码才能观察其行为，这会为我们的反馈循环增加大量时间。

sentences.zh: 更困难的是，当错误的原因发生在其出现之前时，即数据已损坏，但我们没有立即注意到它。

sentences.en: Our usual tools focus on capturing the state of the system at the time a crash happens, which doesn’t tell us why the corruption happened in the first place, just that it happened.
sentences.zh: 我们常用的工具专注于捕获崩溃发生时系统的状态，这并不能告诉我们腐败最初发生的原因，而只是告诉我们它发生了。

blocks.html_en: By adding additional logging, they found the subscribe command was being delivered to ActionCable but wasn’t being received by Redis. Other developers also reported WebSockets often failing in development, indicating it was not only the test environment that was impacted.

sentences.en: By adding additional logging, they found the subscribe command was being delivered to ActionCable but wasn’t being received by Redis.
sentences.zh: 通过添加额外的日志记录，他们发现订阅命令已传递到 ActionCable，但 Redis 未接收到。

sentences.en: Other developers also reported WebSockets often failing in development, indicating it was not only the test environment that was impacted.
sentences.zh: 其他开发人员还报告说 WebSocket 在开发中经常失败，这表明受影响的不仅仅是测试环境。

blocks.html_en: At the end of the third day of debugging, seemingly getting nowhere, Patrick got a very unusual error message, which he posted to Slack with the departing words “I think it’s time to give up for the night…”:

sentences.en: At the end of the third day of debugging, seemingly getting nowhere, Patrick got a very unusual error message, which he posted to Slack with the departing words “I think it’s time to give up for the night…”:
sentences.zh: 在第三天的调试结束时，帕特里克似乎一无所获，他收到了一条非常不寻常的错误消息，他将其发布到 Slack 上，并附上临别词“我认为是时候放弃今晚了……”：

blocks.html_en: text

This kind of exception is quite unusual and hints at how deep this bug goes. But the team hadn’t quite gathered enough information to recognise how or even if it related to the failure. Like an installment of the blockbuster movie series Knives Out, even with all the information, it still didn’t make sense.

sentences.en: This kind of exception is quite unusual and hints at how deep this bug goes.
sentences.zh: 这种异常非常不寻常，并且暗示了这个错误的严重程度。

sentences.en: But the team hadn’t quite gathered enough information to recognise how or even if it related to the failure.
sentences.zh: 但团队还没有收集到足够的信息来识别它与失败有何关系，甚至是否与失败有关。

sentences.en: Like an installment of the blockbuster movie series Knives Out, even with all the information, it still didn’t make sense.
sentences.zh: 就像大片系列《利刃出鞘》的分集一样，即使有所有信息，它仍然没有意义。

blocks.html_en: And yet, with all the pieces on the table, this crime still truly appears impossible. ~ Benoit Blanc

sentences.en: And yet, with all the pieces on the table, this crime still truly appears impossible. ~ Benoit Blanc
sentences.zh: 然而，尽管所有的事情都摆在桌面上，这种犯罪似乎仍然是不可能的。 〜伯努瓦·布兰克

blocks.html_en: And yet, with all the pieces on the table, this crime still truly appears impossible. ~ Benoit Blanc

sentences.en: And yet, with all the pieces on the table, this crime still truly appears impossible. ~ Benoit Blanc
sentences.zh: 然而，尽管所有的事情都摆在桌面上，这种犯罪似乎仍然是不可能的。 〜伯努瓦·布兰克

blocks.html_en: Chapter 3: Everlong

sentences.en: Chapter 3: Everlong
sentences.zh: 第3章 永恒

blocks.html_en:
Hello
I’ve waited here for you
Everlong

sentences.en: Hello I’ve waited here for you Everlong
sentences.zh: 你好，我在这里等你 Everlong

blocks.html_en: Successful debugging requires a good dose of skill and a pinch of luck. Late on the following Thursday afternoon, a message was posted in our engineering Slack channel that a build had triggered a segmentation fault. Attached to the build was a core dump.

sentences.en: Successful debugging requires a good dose of skill and a pinch of luck.
sentences.zh: 成功的调试需要良好的技巧和一点运气。

sentences.en: Late on the following Thursday afternoon, a message was posted in our engineering Slack channel that a build had triggered a segmentation fault.
sentences.zh: 接下来的星期四下午晚些时候，我们的工程 Slack 频道中发布了一条消息，称构建触发了分段错误。

sentences.en: Attached to the build was a core dump.
sentences.zh: 附加到构建的是一个核心转储。

blocks.html_en: The core dump was attached because one of our developers was previously trying to debug a crash in the test suite, and had written a plugin to look for the presence of a core dump and, if one was found, upload it to the relevant build as an artifact.

sentences.en: The core dump was attached because one of our developers was previously trying to debug a crash in the test suite, and had written a plugin to look for the presence of a core dump and, if one was found, upload it to the relevant build as an artifact.
sentences.zh: 附加核心转储是因为我们的一位开发人员之前尝试调试测试套件中的崩溃，并编写了一个插件来查找是否存在核心转储，如果找到，则将其作为工件上传到相关构建。

blocks.html_en: That means it’s time to introduce our third character, Rian, who during his tenure at Buildkite developed a reputation for finding and fixing incredibly obscure bugs (along with the infamous Doom pipeline). The stories are so great that despite Rian’s departure we tell them to new hires, who retell them to others and so on and so forth.

sentences.en: That means it’s time to introduce our third character, Rian, who during his tenure at Buildkite developed a reputation for finding and fixing incredibly obscure bugs (along with the infamous Doom pipeline).
sentences.zh: 这意味着是时候介绍我们的第三个角色 Rian，他在 Buildkite 任职期间因发现和修复令人难以置信的晦涩错误（以及臭名昭著的 Doom 管道）而享有盛誉。

sentences.en: The stories are so great that despite Rian’s departure we tell them to new hires, who retell them to others and so on and so forth.
sentences.zh: 这些故事是如此精彩，以至于尽管 Rian 离开了，我们还是把它们讲给新员工听，新员工又把这些故事再讲给其他人等等。

blocks.html_en: The core dump enabled Rian to inspect the state of the process at the time it crashed, akin to a black box in an aircraft crash investigation. The crash message itself indicated it happened inside the hiredis code, which is a C extension bundled into the redis-client gem. From this, he ascertained that the exception was triggered by a call to the C function memmove. While the source and destination addresses for this call looked sane, the size field was set to 0x00a0ffffffffffb6 , which is about 45 petabytes.

sentences.en: The core dump enabled Rian to inspect the state of the process at the time it crashed, akin to a black box in an aircraft crash investigation.
sentences.zh: 核心转储使 Rian 能够检查坠机时进程的状态，类似于飞机失事调查中的黑匣子。

sentences.en: The crash message itself indicated it happened inside the hiredis code, which is a C extension bundled into the redis-client gem.
sentences.zh: 崩溃消息本身表明它发生在hiredis代码中，该代码是捆绑到redis-client gem中的C扩展。

sentences.en: From this, he ascertained that the exception was triggered by a call to the C function memmove.
sentences.zh: 由此，他确定该异常是由调用 C 函数 memmove 触发的。

sentences.en: While the source and destination addresses for this call looked sane, the size field was set to 0x00a0ffffffffffb6 , which is about 45 petabytes.
sentences.zh: 虽然此调用的源地址和目标地址看起来正常，但大小字段设置为 0x00a0ffffffffffb6 ，约为 45 PB。

blocks.html_en: Based on this and the fact we’d been seeing other unexpected behaviour since upgrading the Redis gem, it was logical to conclude a memory corruption bug was present in the newest version.

sentences.en: Based on this and the fact we’d been seeing other unexpected behaviour since upgrading the Redis gem, it was logical to conclude a memory corruption bug was present in the newest version.
sentences.zh: 基于此，以及自升级 Redis gem 以来我们一直看到其他意外行为的事实，可以合理地得出最新版本中存在内存损坏错误的结论。

blocks.html_en: By another stroke of luck, Rian had attended a conference talk earlier in the year called Finding and fixing memory safety bugs in C with ASAN.

sentences.en: By another stroke of luck, Rian had attended a conference talk earlier in the year called Finding and fixing memory safety bugs in C with ASAN.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，Rian 在今年早些时候参加了一次名为“与 ASAN 一起查找和修复 C 中的内存安全错误”的会议演讲。

blocks.html_en: With this information, Rian set to work getting the reproducible code running in a version of Ruby compiled with ASAN. It only took a couple of hours to get this running, and within 30 minutes ASAN reported a heap use-after-free. In order to provide concurrent execution, hiredis has two threads for each connection, one for reading and one for writing. The use-after-free was happening because in some scenarios the reader thread would free the writer buffer.

sentences.en: With this information, Rian set to work getting the reproducible code running in a version of Ruby compiled with ASAN.
sentences.zh: 有了这些信息，Rian 开始着手让可重现的代码在使用 ASAN 编译的 Ruby 版本中运行。

sentences.en: It only took a couple of hours to get this running, and within 30 minutes ASAN reported a heap use-after-free.
sentences.zh: 运行起来只花了几个小时，30 分钟内 ASAN 报告了堆释放后使用情况。

sentences.en: In order to provide concurrent execution, hiredis has two threads for each connection, one for reading and one for writing.
sentences.zh: 为了提供并发执行，hiredis 为每个连接提供两个线程，一个用于读取，一个用于写入。

sentences.en: The use-after-free was happening because in some scenarios the reader thread would free the writer buffer.
sentences.zh: 发生释放后使用是因为在某些情况下读取器线程会释放写入器缓冲区。

blocks.html_en: Epilogue: In the End

sentences.en: Epilogue: In the End
sentences.zh: 尾声：最后

blocks.html_en:
I’m surprised it got so far
Things aren’t the way they were before
You wouldn’t even recognize me anymore
Not that you knew me back then, but it all comes back to me in the end

sentences.en: I’m surprised it got so far Things aren’t the way they were before You wouldn’t even recognize me anymore Not that you knew me back then, but it all comes back to me in the end
sentences.zh: 我很惊讶事情发展到了这一步，事情已经不再是以前的样子了，你甚至不再认出我了，并不是说你当时认识我，但这一切最终都回到了我的身边

blocks.html_en: Once we knew that there was memory corruption and, more importantly, where it was coming from, fixing it was relatively straightforward. Rian disabled using the C extension for Redis connections in test, development and production, and developed a reproducible test case that was reported upstream.

sentences.en: Once we knew that there was memory corruption and, more importantly, where it was coming from, fixing it was relatively straightforward.
sentences.zh: 一旦我们知道存在内存损坏，更重要的是，知道它来自哪里，修复它就相对简单了。

sentences.en: Rian disabled using the C extension for Redis connections in test, development and production, and developed a reproducible test case that was reported upstream.
sentences.zh: Rian 在测试、开发和生产中禁用了 Redis 连接的 C 扩展，并开发了一个在上游报告的可重现的测试用例。

blocks.html_en: What enabled us to find it in the first place is the interesting part. A combination of deliberate application of our skills in building the necessary tooling, teamwork, and just the right amount of luck. Our tools, such as Test Engine and our coredump-artifact plugin, enabled us to identify the nature of the bug and, combined with using ASAN, where it manifested. Memory corruption bugs such as these can often take months to fix, because having a core dump generated by a segfault only tells you that memory was corrupted at some point in the past, but gives you few clues as to how it became corrupt.

sentences.en: What enabled us to find it in the first place is the interesting part.
sentences.zh: 首先让我们找到它的是有趣的部分。

sentences.en: A combination of deliberate application of our skills in building the necessary tooling, teamwork, and just the right amount of luck.
sentences.zh: 刻意运用我们的技能来构建必要的工具、团队合作和恰到好处的运气。

sentences.en: Our tools, such as Test Engine and our coredump-artifact plugin, enabled us to identify the nature of the bug and, combined with using ASAN, where it manifested.
sentences.zh: 我们的工具（例如测试引擎和 coredump-artifact 插件）使我们能够识别错误的性质，并结合使用 ASAN 来确定错误的表现位置。

sentences.en: Memory corruption bugs such as these can often take months to fix, because having a core dump generated by a segfault only tells you that memory was corrupted at some point in the past, but gives you few clues as to how it became corrupt.
sentences.zh: 诸如此类的内存​​损坏错误通常需要几个月的时间才能修复，因为由段错误生成的核心转储只能告诉您内存在过去的某个时刻已损坏，但几乎无法提供有关内存如何损坏的线索。

blocks.html_en: No production data was harmed in the making of this story.

sentences.en: No production data was harmed in the making of this story.
sentences.zh: 在制作这个故事的过程中，没有任何生产数据受到损害。

content_html_zh:
即使 bug 确实发生，也很难修复它；复制触发它的环境通常是最困难的一步。 当错误是不确定的时，这会变得更加困难：我们需要多次执行重现该错误的代码才能观察其行为，这会为我们的反馈循环增加大量时间。 更困难的是，当错误的原因发生在其出现之前时，即数据已损坏，但我们没有立即注意到它。 我们常用的工具专注于捕获崩溃发生时系统的状态，这并不能告诉我们腐败最初发生的原因，而只是告诉我们它发生了。

这是我们在 redis 客户端库中发现的内存损坏错误以及帮助我​​们实现这一目标的故事。

第一章：弹跳

跳！ 弹跳！ 向下！ 向上！

我们的故事始于我们可扩展性团队的工程经理 David。 在一个闲暇的星期二，大卫发现他的一个构建由于一些看似不稳定的测试而失败了。 他在 Slack 中启动了一个线程来提醒其他人，并附上了测试引擎仪表板的屏幕截图。

作为一家持续集成公司，我们非常熟悉片状测试——它们是任何大型代码库不可避免的一部分。 15分钟内，团队拉动了Andon绳索；这些测试非常不稳定，以至于阻止我们将代码部署到生产环境中。 正如 David 总结的那样，“优先事项是让主要内容畅通无阻”，因此我们努力暂时将测试从我们的套件中排除。

随着紧迫性的降低，大卫和许多其他被这个问题阻碍的开发人员可以自由地回到原来的任务。 不过，David 决定通过使用 Buildkite 的测试引擎可靠性评分检查这些测试的可靠性来进行更深入的研究，看看当它们突然变得不稳定时他是否可以关联起来。

似乎三个最糟糕的问题从 ~100% 可靠变成了…… 不是[2-4天前]左右。 我查看了合并的 PR，看看是否能发现可能促成这一变化的因素，但看不到任何明显的东西…… 唯一跳出来的是redis升级？

Redis 升级是在前周五下午由一名资深工程师兼兼职博客文章叙述者帕特里克·罗宾逊 (Patrick Robinson) 完成的。 帕特里克最喜欢用第三人称谈论自己。其中之一就是深入棘手的错误。

这是我们的第一个线索：Redis gem 升级进展顺利，没有任何生产问题的迹象。 然而，此时出现了第二个假设：测试是不稳定的，因为它们依赖于特定的事件序列。 看，片状测试是我们功能测试套件的一部分；他们不仅使用 RSpec，还使用 ​​Selenium 无头浏览器和由 Web/API 服务器、数据库和 Redis 服务器组成的测试环境。 在这些情况下，不同组件之间的竞争条件是不稳定测试的常见原因。 不幸的是，这会让我们的调查人员偏离轨道。

因此，建议是在测试设置和断言之间休息一下，看看是否可以解决问题。 这不是一个解决方案，而是一种确定不稳定是否是由于 WebSocket 消息到达时间稍长而导致的方法。

第 2 章：淡入黑色

生命似乎会渐渐消逝 一天比一天渐行渐远 迷失在自己的内心 一切都不重要，别人也不重要

接下来的周一，更多测试在与被跳过的文件相同的文件中偶尔失败。 帕特里克与我们的另一位工程师一起工作，与帕特里克不同，他可以编写实际的前端代码。 他们共同成功地构建了该 bug 的可靠重现，尽管花费了长达 30 分钟的时间才出现故障。 在消除了测试套件运行后传递消息的竞争条件的可能性后，团队缩小了 ActionCable 和 Redis 之间的交互范围。

通过添加额外的日志记录，他们发现订阅命令已传递到 ActionCable，但 Redis 未接收到。 其他开发人员还报告说 WebSocket 在开发中经常失败，这表明受影响的不仅仅是测试环境。

在第三天的调试结束时，帕特里克似乎一无所获，他收到了一条非常不寻常的错误消息，他将其发布到 Slack 上，并附上临别词“我认为是时候放弃今晚了……”：

text

ruby(29632,0x2a9f1f000) malloc: Double free of object 0x2a9bb6740
ruby(29632,0x2a9f1f000) malloc: *** set a breakpoint in malloc_error_break to debug

这种异常非常不寻常，并且暗示了这个错误的严重程度。 但团队还没有收集到足够的信息来识别它与失败有何关系，甚至是否与失败有关。 就像大片系列《利刃出鞘》的分集一样，即使有所有信息，它仍然没有意义。

然而，尽管所有的事情都摆在桌面上，这种犯罪似乎仍然是不可能的。 〜伯努瓦·布兰克

第3章 永恒

你好，我在这里等你 Everlong

成功的调试需要良好的技巧和一点运气。 接下来的星期四下午晚些时候，我们的工程 Slack 频道中发布了一条消息，称构建触发了分段错误。 附加到构建的是一个核心转储。

附加核心转储是因为我们的一位开发人员之前尝试调试测试套件中的崩溃，并编写了一个插件来查找是否存在核心转储，如果找到，则将其作为工件上传到相关构建。

这意味着是时候介绍我们的第三个角色 Rian，他在 Buildkite 任职期间因发现和修复令人难以置信的晦涩错误（以及臭名昭著的 Doom 管道）而享有盛誉。 这些故事是如此精彩，以至于尽管 Rian 离开了，我们还是把它们讲给新员工听，新员工又把这些故事再讲给其他人等等。

核心转储使 Rian 能够检查坠机时进程的状态，类似于飞机失事调查中的黑匣子。 崩溃消息本身表明它发生在hiredis代码中，该代码是捆绑到redis-client gem中的C扩展。 由此，他确定该异常是由调用 C 函数 memmove 触发的。 虽然此调用的源地址和目标地址看起来正常，但大小字段设置为 0x00a0ffffffffffb6 ，约为 45 PB。

基于此，以及自升级 Redis gem 以来我们一直看到其他意外行为的事实，可以合理地得出最新版本中存在内存损坏错误的结论。

幸运的是，Rian 在今年早些时候参加了一次名为“与 ASAN 一起查找和修复 C 中的内存安全错误”的会议演讲。

有了这些信息，Rian 开始着手让可重现的代码在使用 ASAN 编译的 Ruby 版本中运行。 运行起来只花了几个小时，30 分钟内 ASAN 报告了堆释放后使用情况。 为了提供并发执行，hiredis 为每个连接提供两个线程，一个用于读取，一个用于写入。 发生释放后使用是因为在某些情况下读取器线程会释放写入器缓冲区。

尾声：最后

我很惊讶事情发展到了这一步，事情已经不再是以前的样子了，你甚至不再认出我了，并不是说你当时认识我，但这一切最终都回到了我的身边

一旦我们知道存在内存损坏，更重要的是，知道它来自哪里，修复它就相对简单了。 Rian 在测试、开发和生产中禁用了 Redis 连接的 C 扩展，并开发了一个在上游报告的可重现的测试用例。

首先让我们找到它的是有趣的部分。 刻意运用我们的技能来构建必要的工具、团队合作和恰到好处的运气。 我们的工具（例如测试引擎和 coredump-artifact 插件）使我们能够识别错误的性质，并结合使用 ASAN 来确定错误的表现位置。 诸如此类的内存​​损坏错误通常需要几个月的时间才能修复，因为由段错误生成的核心转储只能告诉您内存在过去的某个时刻已损坏，但几乎无法提供有关内存如何损坏的线索。

在制作这个故事的过程中，没有任何生产数据受到损害。
